Mitigation: Explicitly frame all conclusions as an analysis of the 'matched machinery subset' and color unmatched villages gray.
Rely on strict string matching to prevent false positives; accept smaller n over corrupted joins.
Avoid causal language. Use terms like 'associated with' rather than 'caused by'.
Enforce strict review of all presentation slides to scrub words like 'reduces' or 'impacts'.
Map unmatched villages as 'Data Not Available' rather than 0 machinery to prevent misleading officials.
Use historical shapefile mapping that aligns with the year the registry data was collected.
Rely on categorical density (In-Situ vs Ex-Situ) rather than strict sum totals where possible.
Aggregate satellite pixels to village polygon levels to smooth over sub-village georeferencing errors.
